web_results.title: US strikes Iran by February 27, 2026?
web_results.description: Propose resolutionThis market will resolve to "Yes" if the US initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate between the time of this market's creation and the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

web_results.title: 2026 United States military buildup in the Middle East - Wikipedia
web_results.description: It was not clear if Trump would make a decision by the weekend. On February 19, 2026, the US could launch military strikes on Iran within days, according to reports, as the White House issued a stark warning that Tehran should make a deal.

web_results.title: Iran Update, February 20, 2026 | ISW
web_results.description: Iran is unlikely to make any meaningful nuclear concessions in its upcoming draft proposal to the United States. Iran may calculate that it can delay the strikes if it offers a sufficiently conciliatory proposal, however. US President Donald Trump gave Iran on February 20 a deadline of “10 to 15 days” to agree to a nuclear deal.

news_results.title: Iran Update, February 24, 2026 | ISW
news_results.description: Iran is seeking support from US adversaries Russia and the People’s Republic of China (PRC) to prepare for war against the United States.

news_results.title: A timeline of tensions over Iran's nuclear program as talks with U.S. approach | PBS News
news_results.description: Iran and the United States are due to hold new talks in Geneva on Thursday over Tehran's nuclear program.

news_results.title: Over 150 U.S. aircraft sweep into Europe, Middle East as Trump mulls strikes
news_results.description: The buildup comes after a round of nuclear talks between the two nations concluded last week without a deal.

news_results.title: US re-asserts 2025 strikes ‘obliterated’ Iran’s nuclear programme | Politics News | Al Jazeera
news_results.description: The White House comment comes after a senior Trump aide said Iran is a week away from having material for nuclear bomb.

news_results.title: A new U.S. attack on Iran could risk large-scale retaliation
news_results.description: If Iran’s leaders believe U.S. strikes threaten their hold on power, they may opt to launch large-scale retaliation aimed at causing American casualties or disrupting the oil industry in the Persian Gulf, former U.S. officials, foreign diplomats and experts say.
